product_overview: CriptEnv is a zero-knowledge secret management platform providing CLI-first workflows, a web dashboard, API-backed synchronization, and CI/CD integrations. All secret encryption/decryption happens client-side (AES-256-GCM) so the server never receives plaintext secrets.
core_goals: Enforce zero-knowledge secret handling: clients encrypt/decrypt; server never receives plaintext.
Provide CLI-first workflows for local vaults and remote sync (push/pull).
Expose secure API surface for projects, environments, secrets, members, CI tokens, and API keys.
Support team collaboration with invites, roles, and audit logs.
Support CI/CD with scoped tokens and a GitHub Action for injecting secrets into pipelines.
Support secret lifecycle features: expiration, rotation, and webhook notifications.
Adopt secure defaults: AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 master derivation, HKDF-SHA256 environment keys.

features.name: Authentication (signup / login / session)
features.description: User account creation and session management using session tokens. Sessions are issued by the API and used to authorize subsequent requests.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/signup with {email, password} -> Receive 201 with user_id
POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with session_token in response body
GET /api/profile with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 with user profile
POST /api/auth/logout with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 204 no content
POST /api/auth/login with incorrect password -> Receive 401 with error
POST /api/auth/login with missing fields -> Receive 400 validation error
GET /api/profile without Authorization header -> Receive 401 unauthorized

features.name: Projects and Environments CRUD
features.description: Create, read, update, and delete projects and environments that group vault data and scope access.
user_flows: POST /api/projects with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {name, description} -> Receive 201 with project_id
GET /api/projects with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 list of projects
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/environments with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {name} -> Receive 201 with env_id
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/environments with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 list of environments
POST /api/projects without Authorization -> Receive 401 unauthorized
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/environments with duplicate name -> Receive 409 conflict

features.name: Encrypted Vault Push & Pull (sync)
features.description: Clients push and pull an encrypted vault blob for a given project + environment. The server stores only encrypted blobs.
user_flows: POST /api/vaults/{project_id}/{env_id}/push with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {encrypted_blob} -> Receive 200 ack
GET /api/vaults/{project_id}/{env_id}/pull with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 with {encrypted_blob}
POST /api/vaults/{project_id}/{env_id}/push with plaintext_blob -> Receive 400 server rejects plaintext
GET /api/vaults/{project_id}/{env_id}/pull without Authorization -> Receive 401 unauthorized

features.name: Secrets CRUD (metadata via API)
features.description: Manage individual secret metadata (encrypted values stored as blobs). APIs return metadata and encrypted payloads; clients decrypt locally.
user_flows: POST /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {env_id, key, encrypted_value, metadata} -> Receive 201 with secret_id
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets?env={env_id} with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 list of secret metadata (no plaintext)
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets/{secret_id} with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 with {encrypted_value, metadata}
DELETE /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets/{secret_id} with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 204 no content
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets with unencrypted value -> Receive 400 validation error
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets/{invalid_secret_id} with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 404 not found

features.name: Team Members and Invites
features.description: Invite collaborators to projects, accept invites, manage roles and revoke access.
user_flows: POST /api/projects/{project_id}/invites with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {email, role} -> Receive 201 with invite_id and invite_token
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/invites/{invite_id}/accept with Authorization: Bearer <session_token_of_invitee> -> Receive 200 with member_id
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/members with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 list of members and roles
DELETE /api/projects/{project_id}/members/{member_id} with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 204 no content
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/invites by non-owner with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 403 forbidden
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/invites/{invite_id}/accept with invalid invite_token -> Receive 400 invalid invite

features.name: CI Tokens and CI Authentication
features.description: Create scoped CI tokens for machines and CI systems; CI systems authenticate and use short-lived sessions to pull encrypted blobs.
user_flows: POST /api/projects/{project_id}/ci-tokens with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {name, scopes, expires_at} -> Receive 201 with token_id and token_value (one-time)
POST /api/ci/authenticate with {token_value} -> Receive 200 with short_lived_session_token
GET /api/vaults/{project_id}/{env_id}/pull with Authorization: Bearer <short_lived_session_token> -> Receive 200 with {encrypted_blob}
POST /api/ci/authenticate with revoked token_value -> Receive 401 unauthorized
GET /api/vaults/{project_id}/{env_id}/pull with expired short_lived_session_token -> Receive 401 unauthorized

features.name: API Keys (public API access)
features.description: Create and validate API keys for programmatic access with scopes and revocation support.
user_flows: POST /api/api-keys with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {name, scopes} -> Receive 201 with key_id and key_value
POST /api/api-keys/validate with {key_value} -> Receive 200 {valid: true, scopes: [...] }
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets?env={env}&api_key={key_value} -> Receive 200 list if key scopes permit
POST /api/api-keys/validate with malformed_key -> Receive 401 invalid key

features.name: Audit Logs
features.description: Store and query audit events for security-sensitive operations such as pushes, rotates, invites, and token usage.
user_flows: GET /api/projects/{project_id}/audit-logs with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 list of audit events
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/audit-logs?since=<timestamp>&type=secret.push with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 filtered events
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/audit-logs without sufficient role with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 403 forbidden

features.name: Secret Expiration & Rotation
features.description: Configure secret expiration metadata and trigger/perform rotations. Expiration state is surfaced to the UI (ExpirationBadge) and can trigger webhooks.
user_flows: POST /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets/{secret_id}/rotate with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 rotation_started
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets/{secret_id}?include_expiry=true with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 with expiry and rotation metadata
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets/{secret_id}/rotate for non-existent secret -> Receive 404 not found
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets/{secret_id}/rotate without proper role -> Receive 403 forbidden

features.name: Webhook Notification Service
features.description: Register webhooks for project events and view delivery events; webhooks are triggered server-side on relevant events (push, rotate, expiry).
user_flows: POST /api/projects/{project_id}/webhooks with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {url, events} -> Receive 201 with webhook_id
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> and {encrypted_value} -> Receive 200 and webhook queued for delivery
GET /api/webhooks/{webhook_id}/events with Authorization: Bearer <session_token> -> Receive 200 list of delivery attempts and statuses
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/webhooks with invalid_url -> Receive 400 validation error
GET /api/webhooks/{webhook_id}/events without Authorization -> Receive 401 unauthorized

features.name: GitHub Action / CI Integration (fetch secrets for pipelines)
features.description: GitHub Action authenticates to the API using a CI token, fetches encrypted secrets for an environment, and the action decrypts them locally to set environment variables.
user_flows: POST /api/ci/authenticate with {ci_token_value} -> Receive 200 with short_lived_session_token
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets?env={env_id}&format=github_actions with Authorization: Bearer <short_lived_session_token> -> Receive 200 with {encrypted_blob_or_payload}
POST /api/ci/authenticate with invalid_ci_token -> Receive 401 unauthorized
GET /api/projects/{project_id}/secrets?env={env_id}&format=github_actions with token lacking scope -> Receive 403 forbidden

project.name: CriptEnv
project.description: Zero-knowledge secret management platform for CLI, web dashboard, API, and CI usage.

api.database: PostgreSQL via SQLAlchemy async and asyncpg
key_features: Encrypted vault blob push and pull
Push and pull sync with API

web.framework: Vinext with React 19 and Next.js-compatible app router

tests.notes: Minimal component tests exist, but package.json has no test script.

key_features: Export secrets as GitHub Actions environment variables

sensitive_rules: Server should never receive plaintext secrets.
Crypto store must not be persisted to localStorage.
Do not log plaintext secret values.
Session token response and localStorage storage are known P0 issues.

current_status.active_focus: Complete ExpirationBadge integration in the web secrets UI.

make_test.failure_pattern: API async tests are not recognized because pytest-asyncio is missing from the API test environment.
make_test.inferred_cause: apps/api/requirements.txt and Makefile install pytest but not pytest-asyncio for API tests.
